28C EEPROM Programmer
  • Shift Register Module
  • View page source

Shift Register Module

Shift register control logic for 28Cx EEPROM programmer.

Contains functions for setting up and controlling the shift register. This includes pin initialization and any low-level control functions needed to interface with the shift register.

Functions

void setupShiftRegisterPins()

Initialize all 74HC595 shift register control pins.

Sets all shift register pins to OUTPUT mode and initializes them to safe states. Clears the shift register on startup to ensure a known state. This function should be called once during system initialization.

See also

clearShiftRegister()

Note

Must be called before any shift register operations

void clearShiftRegister()
void setAddress(uint16_t address)

Set the address lines on the EEPROM using the shift register.

This function shifts out the address to the 74HC595 shift register(s) to set the address lines on the EEPROM. The implementation depends on the hardware configuration and how the shift registers are connected to the EEPROM address lines.

Parameters:

address – The 16-bit address to set on the EEPROM
